Audi|e-tron GT (J1, 2022-2026).decision_summary: Issue #975 official-source review confirmed the EU BEV variant mapping for Audi e-tron GT J1: the supported base variant is `e-tron GT quattro`, and the standalone `e-tron GT` alias was removed from the car library. Ratio fields remain unchanged because this issue verified variant naming and motor layout, not EV reduction-ratio mapping into the current schema.

unresolved.item: Audi e-tron GT (J1, 2022-2026) variant-level final_drive_ratio and top_gear_ratio confirmation
unresolved.reason: Official Audi sources confirmed the supported EU variant set and dual-motor AWD layout, but this issue did not remap EV reduction data into the current final_drive_ratio/top_gear_ratio schema.

issue_975_official_variant_mapping.note: Audi MediaCenter model hub confirms the current e-tron GT family and treats `e-tron GT quattro` as the named base variant.
Official Audi UK model page lists the base model as `e-tron GT quattro`, with no distinct standalone `e-tron GT` variant page.
Official technical data PDF for `e-tron GT quattro` confirms all-wheel drive with two electric motors for the supported base variant.

Audi|Q4 e-tron (FZ, 2022-2026).decision_summary: Issue #975 official-source review confirmed the supported EU Q4 e-tron BEV variant mapping, and this pass adds exact Audi official evidence that the launch-era 50 e-tron quattro uses axle-split one-speed reduction ratios (rear 11.5:1, front 10.0:1) plus mixed-size tires with a wider rear axle. The broad row remains unchanged because the current schema cannot safely encode the axle-split EV ratio mapping and the later official lineup renames the upper quattro variant away from the original 50 e-tron quattro naming.

unresolved.item: Encode axle-split EV reduction ratios for the exact Q4 50 e-tron quattro in repo schema
unresolved.reason: Official Audi sources now prove rear 11.5:1 and front 10.0:1 one-speed reduction ratios for the exact launch-era 50 e-tron quattro, but the current broad row stores only one final_drive_ratio field and cannot safely represent the axle split.

unresolved.item: Exact official numeric tire sizes and post-2024 naming continuity for the broad Q4 e-tron row
unresolved.reason: Official Audi launch material confirms mixed-size tires with a wider rear axle, while later official updates rename the upper quattro variant to 55 e-tron quattro, so this pass did not project one exact tire or ratio package across the full 2022-2026 broad row.

issue_975_official_variant_mapping.note: Official Audi Technology Portal page confirms Q4 40 e-tron as rear-wheel drive with one rear motor and Q4 50 e-tron quattro as dual-motor all-wheel drive.
Audi MediaCenter drivetrain page documents the Q4 e-tron launch mix and confirms there is no non-quattro `Q4 50 e-tron` RWD variant in the supported EU set.

official_50quattro_exact_axle_ratios.note: Official Audi MediaCenter launch drivetrain page for the exact Q4 50 e-tron quattro: dual-motor AWD with one-speed reduction ratios rear 11.5:1 and front 10.0:1.
Official Audi Technology Portal cross-check for the exact Q4 50 e-tron quattro drivetrain layout and the launch-era dual-motor AWD position in the lineup.

Official Audi MediaCenter facts page confirms 19- to 21-inch wheel coverage for the exact launch family and mixed-size tires with a wider rear axle for the quattro variants, though the fetched text did not expose the numeric tire sizes.

Official 2024 Audi update article shows later naming uses 45 e-tron / 45 e-tron quattro / 55 e-tron quattro rather than the original 50 e-tron quattro, so the broad 2022-2026 row should not absorb one exact launch-era ratio package without a split.
